steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes how the Farzan program's performance compares to the road-map method. The sentence then gives two pieces of evidence: it more accurately identified sidewalks, and it distinguished between cobblestone and brick. The blank must capture that the program outperformed the road-map method.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — superior: means higher in quality or better. The evidence directly shows the program bested the road-map method. ✓
  • Choice B — supportive: means giving support or assistance. The program is being compared to the method, not helping it. ✗
  • Choice C — similar: means comparable or alike. The passage says the program was more accurate, not equally accurate. ✗
  • Choice D — changed: means altered or different in form. This describes a transformation, not a quality comparison. ✗
That is the correct answer.
